title: Stelis amaliana
intro: Stelis amaliana is a species of orchid, a type of flowering plant that is native to the tropical regions of Central America. The health benefits of Stelis amaliana include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Stelis amaliana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Stelis amaliana is a species of orchid. Stelis amaliana belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is due to its characteristic orchid morphology and reproductive structures. More specifically, Stelis amaliana is placed within the genus Stelis, which comprises over 1,000 species of orchids. The exact classification of Stelis amaliana may be further detailed through subgenera or tribe-level classification.
